Contenu: This Agreement establishes the legal framework governing the provision of artificial intelligence-based contractual compliance analysis services. The Service Provider shall deliver automated analysis of contractual documents, regulatory comparison, clause extraction, risk classification, and compliance reporting. The AI system may process documents originating from multiple jurisdictions, including the European Union, the United States, Canada, and Morocco. The Parties acknowledge that the system operates through probabilistic models and may generate outputs without deterministic guarantees.

All data processed pursuant to this Agreement shall be hosted exclusively on cloud infrastructure located within the United States of America. The Service Provider does not guarantee data residency within the European Union. Cross-border transfers may occur without additional contractual safeguards beyond standard encryption. The Client acknowledges potential exposure to foreign governmental access requests under applicable U.S. legislation.

The Service Provider may process personal data, industrial data, and compliance-related information necessary to perform the Services. Data may be transferred internationally between jurisdictions without prior case-by-case approval. No Standard Contractual Clauses or equivalent safeguards are automatically incorporated unless separately executed in writing.

The AI system performs automated interpretation of contractual provisions, regulatory cross-referencing, and compliance scoring. Outputs may be delivered without prior human validation. The Service Provider does not guarantee the absence of algorithmic errors, hallucinations, or interpretative inconsistencies. The Client remains responsible for independent legal verification of automated outputs.

The Service Provider shall not be liable for indirect, consequential, regulatory, or reputational damages. Direct liability shall be capped at 5,000 USD irrespective of the actual harm incurred. The Client assumes full responsibility for reliance on AI-generated analyses in regulatory or contractual contexts.

No contractual right to audit the internal logic, source code, training data, or reasoning mechanisms of the AI system is granted. The Service Provider may provide limited descriptive documentation but is not obligated to disclose full reasoning traces or inference logs.

The Service Provider implements commercially reasonable security controls including encryption in transit and at rest. Compliance with specific standards such as ISO/IEC 27001 or NIST SP 800-53 is not guaranteed unless expressly certified. Cybersecurity risks inherent to cloud-based AI services are acknowledged by both Parties.

This Agreement shall be governed exclusively by the laws of the State of Delaware, United States. Any dispute shall be submitted to the competent courts of Delaware. The Parties waive application of conflicting jurisdictional rules except where mandatorily imposed.

Either Party may terminate this Agreement with thirty days written notice. Upon termination, data retention and deletion policies shall be determined by the Service Provider unless otherwise required by applicable law. No guaranteed deletion timeframe is provided.

This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes prior communications. Amendments must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives. Invalid provisions shall not affect the validity of remaining clauses.

The Parties acknowledge that regulatory obligations may evolve due to legislative amendments, judicial interpretations, or administrative guidance. The Service Provider reserves the right to update  internal  AI  processing  mechanisms,  embeddings,  model  architectures,  or  retrieval  strategies without prior notification, provided that service continuity is maintained. The Client agrees that automated outputs are advisory in nature and do not constitute legal opinions. The Parties further recognize that multi-jurisdictional data flows may create complex compliance obligations requiring independent verification by qualified legal professionals.
